ticketTitle: Add comprehensive tests in scorer_test.go: unsupported language → N/A, unmeasured complexity not scored as excellent, Swift-like generic unit produces no fabricated dimensions, and regression tests for each fix area.

nonGoals: Do NOT change production code in scorer.go, language_tiers.go, or anywhere else
Do NOT modify existing test functions
Do NOT touch report generation or init_cmd.go
Do NOT test aggregate behavior — that is a separate deliverable
deliverables.objective: Add a regression test verifying that a supported Go unit with realistic metrics produces a real letter grade and populated dimension scores
deliverables.planText: Add TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks to scorer_test.go. Construct a Go Unit (domain.NewUnit) with realistic CodeMetrics evidence (complexity, coverage, duplication, etc.) and a passing policy.EvaluationResult. Call engine.CertifyUnit. Assert the returned record.Grade is one of A/B/C/D/F (not N/A, not empty), record.Dimensions is non-nil with entries for maintainability, test-quality, etc., and record.Unsupported is false. Use the existing external test package (engine_test) and follow patterns from pipeline_test.go for constructing units with evidence.

nonGoals: Do NOT change production code
Do NOT modify language_tiers.go
Do NOT modify the existing TestScorer_TierGenericComplexitySkipsMaintainability test
Do NOT add new dimension keys or change the scoring model
deliverables.objective: Add a test proving that a TierGeneric unit with zero complexity is never scored as excellent (maintainability ≠ 0.95)
deliverables.planText: Add TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored to scorer_test.go. Build on the existing TestScorer_TierGenericComplexitySkipsMaintainability (line 281) by adding a stronger assertion: construct a TierGeneric Unit (language 'swift' or 'python') with Complexity=0, invoke scoreFromMetrics or Score, and assert that maintainability is NOT 0.95 (the old 'excellent' default). The dimension should either be absent from the map or explicitly marked unmeasured. If testing at Score() level, TierGeneric returns nil — test at the appropriate level where dimensions are observable, or assert nil return and document that the absence of dimensions implies no fabricated excellent score.

nonGoals: Do NOT change production code
Do NOT modify language_tiers.go
Do NOT add or change default values in the scoring engine
Do NOT test aggregate behavior
deliverables.objective: Add a test verifying that TierGeneric units have no default 0.5 dimension values injected
deliverables.planText: Add TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected to scorer_test.go. Construct a TierGeneric Unit (language 'swift', 'python', or empty string) with minimal metrics. Call engine.CertifyUnit. Assert that for every dimension in record.Dimensions, the value is NOT 0.5 (the old hardcoded default). If Score() returns nil for TierGeneric (as per existing behavior), assert record.Dimensions is nil — proving no defaults leak through. Also assert record.Unsupported is true and record.Grade is 'F'. Document in test comments that nil dimensions is the expected guard against default injection.

nonGoals: Do NOT change production code to make Grade='N/A' — this is test-only
Do NOT modify language_tiers.go
Do NOT change the CertifyUnit or Score function signatures
If the N/A assertion cannot pass, document current behavior (Grade F) and add a TODO referencing the desired behavior
deliverables.objective: Add a test documenting how unsupported-language units are handled (nil Score / Grade F), with clear comments on desired vs. current behavior
deliverables.planText: Add TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA to scorer_test.go. Construct a Unit with Language='Swift' (a TierGeneric/unsupported language). The investigation found that engine.Score() returns nil for TierGeneric and engine.CertifyUnit() assigns Grade 'F'. Write the test at the CertifyUnit level: assert record.Unsupported is true and record.Grade is 'F' (documenting current behavior). Add a comment noting that the desired N/A grade would require a production change. Alternatively, test at Score() level: assert Score() returns nil for TierGeneric (strengthening the existing TestScorer_TierGenericReturnsNil). The architect should choose the appropriate level. Include assertions that no dimension scores are present for the unsupported unit.

nonGoals: Do NOT add an aggregate function to scorer.go
Do NOT change production code
Do NOT modify language_tiers.go or report generation
Do NOT create a fake aggregate just to make the test pass
deliverables.objective: Add a test documenting the aggregate-skip behavior for unsupported units, adapting to the absence of an aggregate function
deliverables.planText: Add TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported to scorer_test.go. The investigation found no aggregate function in scorer.go — only WeightedAverage which operates on scores. Options (architect chooses): (a) Skip this test with a comment noting no aggregate function exists yet. (b) Test engine.CertifyUnit isolation: call CertifyUnit on an unsupported unit, assert it returns a record with Unsupported=true and Grade='F', and verify that when iterating results, unsupported units can be filtered out by the Unsupported flag — this documents the intended aggregate-skip pattern for future implementers. (c) Test engine.WeightedAverage with a nil input, asserting it returns 0. Write the chosen approach with clear rationale.
